tool.name: finish_scan
tool.description:
Complete the security scan with a professional penetration test report. REQUIRED for root/main agent only. Subagents must use agent_finish. ALL subagents must be completed before calling this. ALL vulnerabilities must be reported with create_vulnerability_report first (no duplicates). Calling multiple times OVERWRITES the report.
Report rules (PDF-ready): Do NOT include local paths, internal tools, agents, sandboxes, models, system prompts, connection issues, or tester environment details. Tone: formal, objective, third-person, concise. Structure: Executive summary (risk posture, key findings, business impact), Methodology (OWASP/PTES, scope), Technical analysis (consolidated findings from vulnerability reports), Recommendations (prioritized remediation).

parameter.name: executive_summary
parameter.description: High-level summary for non-technical stakeholders. Include: risk posture assessment, key findings in business context, potential business impact (data exposure, compliance, reputation), and an overarching remediation theme. Write in clear, accessible language for executive leadership.

parameter.name: methodology
parameter.description: Testing methodology and scope. Include: frameworks and standards followed (e.g., OWASP WSTG, PTES), engagement type and approach (black-box, gray-box), in-scope assets and target environment, categories of testing activities performed, and evidence validation standards applied.

parameter.name: technical_analysis
parameter.description: Consolidated overview of confirmed findings and risk patterns. Include: severity model used, a high-level summary of each finding with its severity rating, and systemic root causes or recurring themes observed across findings. Reference individual vulnerability reports for reproduction details — do not duplicate full evidence here.

parameter.name: recommendations
parameter.description: Prioritized, actionable remediation guidance organized by urgency (Immediate, Short-term, Medium-term). Each recommendation should provide specific technical remediation steps. Conclude with retest and validation guidance.

returns.description: Response containing success status, vulnerability count, and completion message. If agents are still running, returns details about active agents and suggested actions.

function.parameter: Penetration test of target identified critical SQL injection in the login endpoint allowing unauthenticated database access. Overall risk: Critical. Remediation: prioritize input validation and parameterized queries.
Assessment followed OWASP WSTG methodology. Black-box external pentest of the web application and API. Testing covered authentication, authorization, input handling, and session management.

One confirmed finding: SQL injection (Critical) in the login endpoint. User-supplied input is concatenated directly into SQL queries without sanitization or parameterization. This enables authentication bypass and full database access.

Immediate: Replace string concatenation with parameterized queries/prepared statements. Short-term: Add input validation and WAF rules. Medium-term: Security code review and automated SAST testing in CI/CD.
